description: This image displays physical photographs of forged billets and machined tensile test specimens of Fe-30Mn-9Al-0.8C low-density steel after different warm multiaxial forging (MAF) passes, placed above a metric ruler with centimeter scale for size reference. From left to right, the three groups correspond to samples after 1 MAF pass (MAF-1), 3 MAF passes (MAF-3), and 5 MAF passes (MAF-5) respectively; each group includes 1 forged billet on the top and 2 cut dog-bone shaped tensile specimens at the bottom. All samples are processed via warm MAF at 250 °C, with 33% normal strain per orthogonal compression direction per pass. The steel fractures on the 6th forging attempt, so 5 passes is the maximum achievable processing pass number. This figure confirms successful fabrication of MAF-processed steel samples for subsequent microstructure characterization and mechanical property testing, supporting investigation of plastic flow behavior during warm MAF of this low-density steel.
